$comment: GENERATED STRUCTURE + carried-forward prose. component/kind/source and motionVocabulary are DERIVED FROM CODE by `npm run registry:build` — do NOT hand-edit them. intent/useWhen/avoidWhen/variants ARE hand-authored and are carried forward by component id. After adding a primitive, run `npm run registry:build`; `npm run registry:check` gates drift (regenerate-then-diff).
description: Reusable craft catalog for the remotion-svg-primitives lesson pipeline. Source of truth for SHAPE is src/capabilities/schema.ts; source of truth for WHAT EXISTS is the code (barrels + unions). This JSON is a report-only baseline in cut 1.

primitives.useWhen: A tappable answer or choice card holding a number, text, or icon child, with correct/wrong/selected/focused states and a state badge.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing a bare digit with no answer-choice framing — use NumberCard.

primitives.useWhen: Wrapping a row of ten sticks into a bundle with a rope, band, or ribbon that draws on via wrapProgress to show ten ones becoming one ten.
primitives.avoidWhen: Grouping objects with no physical wrap gesture, or drawing the sticks themselves — use StickGroup with layout=bundle.

primitives.useWhen: Placing a >, <, or = between two quantities, optionally as a hungry-mouth glyph or hidden behind a ? until revealed.
primitives.avoidWhen: Using = inside a built-out equation — use EquationStrip's operator tile.

primitives.useWhen: Proving a bundled ten still contains ten ones: xrayProgress fades the wrap to a ghost outline and reveals the count inner sticks inside, defusing the 'a ten is just one thing now' misconception.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing ten ones BECOMING one ten (the wrap drawing on) rather than the ten still being ten ones — use BundleWrap with wrapProgress.

primitives.useWhen: A single cute countable thing (fish, star, fruit, etc.) to populate a counting or comparison set, with selected/dimmed/label states.
primitives.avoidWhen: Abstract place-value units or sticks — use UnitBlock or SmallStick.

primitives.useWhen: Making 'each next number is the previous plus one' physically visible on a single-rod bead counter (计数器): the displayed number is driven by count (0..capacity), and pulling one more bead onto the rod grows it by exactly one — 1→2→3→4→5. The newest bead (index count-1) slides into its seat via revealProgress (eased) and, when activeIndex points at it, swells a 'just-moved' ring via activePulse, while the value readout flips to the new count — so one more bead and a bigger number are one synchronized beat. Reach for it in any 1~N number-sequence / 接着数 / +1 lesson; horizontal grows left→right, vertical stacks bottom→top; beadColor/rodColor/capacity/beadRadius all vary by prop.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing a fixed-form decorative abacus with no count/progress (use the abacus IconAsset), or counting loose scattered objects with no ordered next-one (use StickGroup/CountStepIndicator). Not for place-value tens/ones (use PlaceValueMat) or a ten-fill grid (use TenFrameRod).

primitives.useWhen: A small circular badge that pops and fades in via progress to mark the running count at each step of a counting sequence.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing a total with an optional unit label — use StepTally; tracking position on a line — use NumberLineTrack.

primitives.useWhen: Laying out a horizontal number sentence (left op right = result) with an optional active or blank tile to spotlight a missing term.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing a part-whole split rather than an arithmetic sentence — use FenHeDiagram or PartWholeBrace.

primitives.useWhen: Showing a whole splitting into two parts (分合式) or composing two parts into a whole; identity-preserving via getFenHeDiagramAnchors.
primitives.avoidWhen: Plain counting or comparison with no part-whole relationship.

primitives.useWhen: Dropping a centered word or phrase with an optional draw-on underline that fades in via progress to name what is on screen.
primitives.avoidWhen: Hand-drawn teacher annotation pointing at a target — use TeacherMark.

primitives.useWhen: Showing a single digit or short value on a rounded card with optional blank line and correct/wrong/selected states, auto-sized so the glyph always insets cleanly.
primitives.avoidWhen: The value is an answer choice with an icon or label — use AnswerTile.

primitives.useWhen: Showing position, ticks, highlights, and animated arc jumps along a min-to-max number line with a current-value badge.
primitives.avoidWhen: Counting discrete objects with no linear scale — use a CountableObject set or StickGroup.

primitives.useWhen: Marking a POSITION (序数) rather than a quantity (基数) with a two-part label whose PREFIX is visually distinct from the digit — a NumberCard surface carrying a caller-supplied prefix glyph (第 or any locale prefix, default none) in a separate prefixColor/prefixWeight beside the value digit. Reach for it whenever the teaching point is 'same number, only the prefix tells position from count': stand a 第N token beside a bare same-height NumberCard so the digit reads identical and ONLY the 第 differs, label members of an ordered queue (第1..第N) above the row, or tag any labeled-sequence position. Prop-driven and lesson-agnostic — value:number|string, prefix:ReactNode, prefixColor/prefixWeight emphasis, color for the digit, selected/focused/correct/wrong states, width/height + x/y/transform placement; bakes no Chinese, value, or topic. Entrance is the caller's PopIn at a cue offset; state is prop-driven (no own animation).
primitives.avoidWhen: The label is a bare cardinal with one fill/weight — use NumberCard (no distinguished prefix). The whole ordered-counting beat (walk the row 1→N, spotlight the Nth, contrast 一共N vs 第N, renumber on a direction flip) is the demand — use OrderedRowSpotlight, which composes its own inline ordinal token. The label is an answer choice with an icon — use AnswerTile.

primitives.useWhen: Drawing a curly brace that reveals via progress to bracket a span as one whole or one part, in any of four directions with an optional label.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing the numeric whole/parts relationship itself — use FenHeDiagram.

primitives.useWhen: Seating place-value contents in labeled tens/ones (optionally hundreds) columns under headers with a divider and optional written digit per column, to bridge a ten-bundle plus loose ones to the digits of a numeral; pass a child per column or perColumnCount placeholders, highlightColumn to focus one.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing bare countables or a ten-structure with no column/digit framing — use a CountableObject set or TenFrameRod.

primitives.useWhen: Splitting ONE filled round region (a cookie/disk) into N equal-area parts to teach halves/thirds/quarters: cutProgress draws the cuts on, highlightPart shades one part, separation pulls the parts out to hand each to someone.
primitives.avoidWhen: Decomposing a NUMBER into addends (use FenHeDiagram) or bracketing a linear span (use PartWholeBrace) — RegionSplit is about dividing a continuous area, not a count.

primitives.useWhen: A single counting stick with idle/active/counted highlight, rotation, and scale — the unit primitive for stick counting and bundling.
primitives.avoidWhen: Placing many sticks at once — use StickGroup, which lays out and highlights them for you.

primitives.useWhen: Showing the running total as a numeric pill or a row of dots, with an optional unit label, that pops in via progress.
primitives.avoidWhen: Marking just the current step with no total — use CountStepIndicator.

primitives.useWhen: Laying out N counting sticks in a scatter, row, or bundle with per-index active highlight, reveal-up-to, and seeded scatter placement.
primitives.avoidWhen: A single stick or a non-stick countable — use SmallStick or CountableObject.

primitives.useWhen: Filling a 10-cell two-row ten-frame or one-row rod by count or colored segments to show how many make ten and the ones inside.
primitives.avoidWhen: Counting loose objects with no ten-structure — use a CountableObject set; physical stick bundling — use StickGroup plus BundleWrap.

primitives.useWhen: Showing 1-10 place-value units as cubes, dots, chips, or a segmented rod, optionally stacked, with a value label.
primitives.avoidWhen: Cute themed countables for early counting — use CountableObject; a structured ten-frame — use TenFrameRod.

primitives.useWhen: Drawing a single hanzi stroke on via durationInFrames or progress, with a faint ghost guide underneath and an optional pen-tip cursor.
primitives.avoidWhen: A generic non-stroke path reveal — use DrawPath; teacher annotation marks — use TeacherMark.

primitives.useWhen: Presenting a Chinese character (one per card; compose several side by side for a contrasting SET — e.g. a tone minimal pair 妈/麻/马/骂) with optional pinyin, word, and a small pictograph hint, in selected/focused states.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing pinyin spelling structure (initial/final/tone) rather than the character — use PinyinSyllableCard.

primitives.useWhen: Marking that a sound or syllable can be played — a small speaker icon whose sound arcs swell in state=playing (driven by progress) to signal audio is available here.
primitives.avoidWhen: Teaching how a sound is articulated (lip position) — use MouthShapeIcon; ListenIcon is an audio affordance, not an articulation teaching icon.

primitives.useWhen: A face icon whose mouth shows the articulation (open, round, smile, teeth) for teaching a pinyin sound's lip position.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing the written syllable or tone — use PinyinSyllableCard or ToneMarkGlyph.

primitives.useWhen: Showing a pinyin syllable split into initial and final tiles plus a tone mark, with the initial, final, or tone segment highlightable.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing the Chinese character itself — use HanziCard; just the tone curve — use ToneMarkGlyph.

primitives.useWhen: Riding a moving dot along a Mandarin tone contour by progress (0..1) to show where the pitch is right now, layered over a ToneMarkGlyph of the same tone and size.
primitives.avoidWhen: Drawing the tone contour shape itself — use ToneMarkGlyph, which draws the stroke this playhead travels along.

primitives.useWhen: A tile holding a single radical or character component with an optional label and correct/wrong/selected answer states.
primitives.avoidWhen: Presenting a full character with pinyin and word — use HanziCard.

primitives.useWhen: A Chinese-writing practice cell (田/米/half grid) with dashed guides and an optional highlighted focus zone for where a stroke goes.
primitives.avoidWhen: Animating the stroke itself — use AnimatedStrokePath inside the cell.

primitives.useWhen: Drawing one of the four Mandarin tone contours (or the neutral dot) that strokes on via progress to teach pitch shape.
primitives.avoidWhen: Showing the full syllable structure around the tone — use PinyinSyllableCard.

primitives.useWhen: Drawing a line that grows via progress between two points to pair or match items, optionally dotted with snap endpoint dots that turn green on completion.
primitives.avoidWhen: An annotated teacher arrow or label line — use TeacherMark or PointerHandArrow.

primitives.useWhen: Pointing the child's attention at a target with an arrow, pointing hand, or sparkle that nudges and scales in via progress, in any of four directions.
primitives.avoidWhen: A hand-drawn annotation stroke or a connecting match line — use TeacherMark or PairConnector.

primitives.useWhen: A star, coin, badge, or node token with a circular progress ring filled by progress, showing a percent or OK once collected.
primitives.avoidWhen: Tracking the count of a teaching set rather than session reward progress — use StepTally.

primitives.useWhen: A labeled basket or tray drop-target whose state (idle/accept/reject) recolors to show whether a sorted item belongs there.
primitives.avoidWhen: Just collecting reward progress, or matching pairs — use RewardProgressToken or PairConnector.

primitives.useWhen: Marking the absence of a pairing partner under a surplus item in a compare-by-matching layout, so 'more than' reads as 'these have nobody' — a dashed ghost of the missing partner or a struck empty slot.
primitives.avoidWhen: The partner EXISTS and you are drawing the match line between two present items — use PairConnector (UnmatchedSlot is for the item that is MISSING a partner).

primitives.useWhen: Hand-drawn teacher ink (underline, wrap-arc, label-arrow, vs-mark) that draws on via drawProgress with optional boil wobble and pen-settle.
primitives.avoidWhen: A clean typeset label or directional pointer — use LabelCallout or PointerHandArrow.

primitives.useWhen: Render a generated+traced flat SVG asset for a fixed-form representational object — a bundle, mascot, prop — that's too complex to hand-code; pass the asset name, placement, scale/size, and (color variant) on-palette theme fills, or variant=mono tinted via a theme token.
primitives.avoidWhen: A PARAMETRIC teaching primitive that must count/morph/preserve identity — hand-code those; IconAsset is fixed-form decorative and cannot vary its count or split into parts.

motionComponents.useWhen: Revealing ONE quantity surviving across an ordered ladder of representations — 实物 → 小棒(1:1) → 圆点 → 数字 — in one continuous left→right (or top→down) reveal, so the SAME count is seen conserved at every step of abstraction and the child stops splitting 'counting objects' from 'the number symbol'. Faint 1:1 connectors tie like-index units between adjacent rungs, and every unit converges on the single numeral card — the 'N个XX用N表示' cardinality payoff. Prop-driven: count:number; stages (caller picks/orders the rungs, defaults the full ladder); objectVariant for the objects rung; atFrame+perStageDurationFrames (cue-relative, the component derives each rung's local progress); orientation row|column; tint; revealLabel per-stage caption nodes (caller passes the localized 'N个XX用N表示' string). A 6~10 lesson, or a same-count-across-different-kinds beat (label 2 geese AND 2 chairs with the same numeral), reuses it unchanged.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: A single static representation (no abstraction step to show) — use the bare primitive (CountableObject / NumberCard); or a ten-ones→one-ten bundling where the units physically GATHER into one object — use AssetMorph; or counts so large the per-rung units fall below the kids-eye min size (keep count <= ~6 for the full 4-rung ladder at 1280-wide). It conserves a count across abstraction levels; it does NOT compose/decompose a whole into parts (use FenHeDiagram) or compare two different quantities (use a paired-column compare).

motionComponents.useWhen: Transform a PARAMETRIC group the child has been reasoning about into its fixed-form generated IconAsset (or back) without a visible cut: a short FX-masked crossfade fires a SparkleBurst over the swap while the arriving element settles, so ten sticks BECOME one roped bundle (direction=bundle) or a bundle un-bundles into fanning sticks (direction=unbundle). Caller supplies from/to and a shared centerX/centerY (bbox-matched so identity is preserved); atFrame is cues[id].startFrame+offset.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: Swapping two things that are NOT the same object (no identity to preserve) — use a plain cut or PopIn; or morphing at multiplicity where each unit needs its own highlight — keep those parametric. The teaching mechanics (the sticks gathering/counting) stay on the parametric from; AssetMorph only owns the masked swap.

motionComponents.useWhen: The whole 'ten ones become one roped ten, that still IS ten ones' regrouping beat as ONE registered unit. Composes AssetMorph (the gathered parametric from BECOMES the held asset, FX-masked) + the held IconAsset + ConservationBundle (the x-ray peek): drive peekProgress 0..1 from the conservation cue and the held bundle crossfades down while the wrap ghosts to reveal the count ones living inside, then re-solidifies. Caller supplies from/asset/centerX/centerY (bbox-matched, like AssetMorph) and the count/colors of the inner ones; morphAtFrame + peekProgress are cue-relative.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: Bundling with NO conservation peek (use AssetMorph alone), or a peek with no preceding morph (use ConservationBundle alone). Multiplicity where each inner one needs its own live highlight during the held phase — assets can't per-index highlight; keep that parametric. The gather-before / fan-after motion stays on the caller's parametric from — this owns only the masked swap + the asset↔x-ray crossfade.

motionComponents.useWhen: Stagger an appendage/child chain with trailing lag.
Revealing any SVG path stroke-by-stroke via durationInFrames or an external progress, using a pathLength dash trick.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: A hanzi stroke needing a ghost guide and cursor — use AnimatedStrokePath; an object traveling along a path — use FollowPath.

motionComponents.useWhen: Moving a child element along a path spec via progress, optionally rotating it to the path tangent, to animate something tracing a route.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: Drawing the path's ink rather than moving along it — use DrawPath.

motionComponents.useWhen: Teaching a child to WRITE a single glyph in the correct stroke order (笔顺) inside a writing cell — a numeral (1/2/3/4/5, or any digit) or a comparison symbol (= > <), or any future glyph. It traces the glyph stroke by stroke IN ORDER: stroke k starts only after stroke k-1 finishes (+ gap), each with a faint ghost-under guide, a moving pen cursor, and a 起笔/收笔 dot whose start marker pulses just before its stroke begins, so direction and order are unmistakable — the fix for the 2的弧线/3的两个半圆 写反/弧度变形 and the 把符号画成图/笔画不匀 misconceptions. Prop-driven and LESSON-AGNOSTIC: it bakes NO glyph — the caller passes the ordered `strokes` (looked up from the shared GLYPH_STROKES data keyed by the glyph char, via `glyphStrokesFor(char)`). `atFrame` is cues[id].startFrame+offset (the writer derives every stroke's local progress by index — ZERO frame literals); `perStrokeDurationFrames` + optional `interStrokeGapFrames` set the pace; `size` scales the normalized 0..100 stroke data to the cell; `grid`/`focusZone` pass through to StrokeGuideCell; `stroke`/`strokeWidth`/`cursor`/`showStartDots` style the pen. COMPOSES StrokeGuideCell (田字格/米字格 backdrop) + one AnimatedStrokePath per stroke (ghost + cursor + progress) + 起笔/收笔 dot markers. Drives 1–5 AND =,>,< AND any glyph with stroke data unchanged.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: Showing a glyph that is already WRITTEN (no stroke-order teaching) — use NumberCard / ComparisonSymbol / a plain text glyph. Tracing a full hanzi character (the GLYPH_STROKES data ships digits 0-9 + comparison symbols; add the character's ordered paths to that shared data first — don't bake them into a scene). Animating a non-writing path reveal (a number-line arc, a teacher underline) — use DrawPath / AnimatedStrokePath / TeacherMark directly. The writer owns ORDERED multi-stroke glyph formation inside a cell; a single decorative draw-on is one AnimatedStrokePath.

motionComponents.useWhen: Teaching the ordinal cluster on ONE ordered row of caller-supplied items (countables, faces, sticks): count the row 1→N with a moving finger (the 摆—数—说—写 count step), mark ONLY the Nth position as 第N (an ordinal spotlight ring + the 第N token), contrast circle-ALL ('一共5', a PartWholeBrace heap) vs spotlight-ONE ('第5'), and RENUMBER the row from the chosen end when a direction arrow flips — the same item gets its mirror position (第2 ↔ 第4) live, teaching relativity / 参照点变化 / turn-around. Prop-driven & LESSON-AGNOSTIC, bakes NO figure or string: items:ReactNode[] (the row members — identity is caller scene content); direction:'ltr'|'rtl' (which END is position 1 — the SINGLE place direction enters; flipping it re-derives every position via posOf=ltr?i+1:n-i); spotlightOrdinal (a 1-based POSITION, so it tracks a flip automatically); showCardinalBracket + cardinalLabel (the '一共N' heap half of the contrast); ordinalLabel:(pos)=>node (caller localizes '第'+pos — never baked); countWalk / pointerIndex (opt-in 1→N finger + running tally; a static panel shows neither); atFrame + stepDurationFrames (cue-relative — the component derives the pointer walk, spotlight pop, and arrow draw-on by index, ZERO frame literals); rowGap/itemRadius/arrowColor/spotlightColor. COMPOSES PointerHandArrow(hand) + StepTally + CountStepIndicator (per-item position numbers) + PartWholeBrace(up) + TeacherMark(label-arrow) + an inline 第N token, with named EASE.* motion. Any N (2..~8), any item kind, either direction, cardinal OR ordinal mode — one component drives 摆数说写, ordinal spotlight, cardinal/ordinal contrast, relativity, and turn-around.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: An UNORDERED set where position is meaningless (pure 'how many' / cardinality across kinds) — use CountableObject rows + AbstractionLadder; comparing TWO different quantities by matching — use getPairedColumnPlacement + PairConnector / UnmatchedSlot; composing/decomposing a whole into parts — use FenHeDiagram; counting a row WITHOUT any ordinal/position claim — a bare CountStepIndicator walk is lighter. Keep N <= ~8 so items + position numbers stay above the kids-eye min size at 1280-wide. It owns the SAME-row ordinal/cardinal/relativity beat; it does NOT teach the digits' stroke order (GlyphStrokeWriter) or a parametric→asset morph (AssetMorph).

motionComponents.useWhen: Entrance scale physics for any appearing element.
Emitting expanding fading ripple rings from a point for a fixed repeatCount to draw a beat of attention to a spot.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: A celebratory star burst — use SparkleBurst; a directed pointer — use PointerHandArrow.

motionComponents.useWhen: Motion-blur substitute for fast straight-line travel (>180 vbu/frame).
Firing a one-shot radial burst of stars outward from a point to celebrate a correct answer or completed beat.
motionComponents.avoidWhen: A sustained attention pulse — use PulseCircle; a persistent reward token — use RewardProgressToken.

fxComponents.useWhen: Moving-hold idle breathing so held elements stay alive.
Render once per scene root to publish the FX filter defs.
fxComponents.avoidWhen: Never omit when any fx/ component is used.

fxComponents.useWhen: Point flash at a moment of emphasis.
Pulsing aura wrapper to draw the eye.
fxComponents.avoidWhen: On more than one element at a time.

fxComponents.useWhen: Cinematic shine sweeping across a rectangle (card/title).
fxComponents.avoidWhen: On teaching primitives the child is reasoning about.

fxComponents.useWhen: One-shot sparkle particle accent on a reward/reveal.
fxComponents.avoidWhen: Under narration or as continuous decoration.

styles.useWhen: Canonical lesson appearance — no aesthetic overlay.
Sumi-e ink on warm rice paper aesthetic overlay.
